sentence_reviews.text: The agent learns or uses a predictive environment model to evaluate and plan future actions.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Sutton & Barto, Reinforcement Learning: An Introduction, 2nd ed., model-based methods chapters/sections on models and planning; AIMA contents.html, Chapter 22 'Reinforcement Learning' and Chapter 26.5 'Planning and Control'.
sentence_reviews.notes: The sources support RL, models/planning, and control context; the exact wording is a concise synthesis rather than a directly quoted definition.

sentence_reviews.text: The chosen learning signal and interaction pattern may not match the deployment objective or data-generating process. Its direct dependencies include Reinforcement learning, Latent variables & hidden state.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Synthesized from cited node sources plus the atlas dependency context in data/atlas.json.
sentence_reviews.notes: Limitation is reviewed as a broad synthesis/tradeoff statement for the family-level node, not as a direct quotation from one source.

sentence_reviews.text: Model-based reinforcement learning follows the learning setup expressed by Reinforcement learning.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: RL book model-based methods as a reinforcement-learning family; AIMA contents.html, Chapter 22 'Reinforcement Learning'.
sentence_reviews.notes: Model-based RL is a specialization of RL, so the trained_under/core relationship is defensible.

sentence_reviews.text: In this atlas graph, Model-based reinforcement learning is modeled as depending on Reinforcement learning, Latent variables & hidden state and feeding downstream nodes such as Latent world models & predictive dynamics, World-model rollout & imagination, Game-playing, control & simulation agents.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: data/atlas.json canonical node and dependency graph; generated from node dependencies and downstream consumers.
sentence_reviews.notes: This is an explicit internal atlas-graph positioning claim, not an external-world necessity claim.
